body:
## Summary

Stacked on #27. Pairs with [self-evolving/comment-api#8](https://github.com/self-evolving/comment-api/pull/8): the drawer experience now ships from the comments service as `sepo.js`, and this PR deletes the template's vendored copy (**+85 / −1928**).

- `Comments.tsx` emits only a hidden `.sepo-embed` config element; `comments.inline.ts` shrinks to forwarding that config onto a `sepo.js` script tag from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` on each SPA navigation (sepo.js is idempotent across re-injection)
- The site's own light/dark toggle drives the widget theme via the new `window.sepoComments.setTheme()` API; `GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME` / `GISCUS_DARK_THEME` default to the service-hosted `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark` (replacing `GISCUS_THEME_URL`)
- Deleted: drawer markup/SCSS/behavior, `quartz/static/giscus/{light,dark}.css`, and the mascot webps — all served by comment-api now

Every existing `GISCUS_*` env (tabs, content repo, `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`, trigger mode) keeps working unchanged.

## Checks

- `npm run check` (tsc + prettier)
- Build with the full env (`GISCUS_APP_HOST=http://localhost:3000`, bot trigger, tabs, content repo): pages emit the `.sepo-embed` config with all attributes; `postscript.js` carries the injection logic
- Merge order note: deploy comment-api#8 before merging this, since the drawer then loads from the service

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

comments.body: [vc]: #mrZv6E+yKKsR5DkEbhTp5iTWTsmi9lbUrO/P8Qvre/8=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/4qr9GWngTPRi9JrqJxZuJKcPxDU4) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-feat-use-sepo-embed-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-feat-use-sepo-embed-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 11, 2026 3:32am |

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 95 | PASS | 12 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | `comments.inline.ts` removes/reuses a stable `sepo-embed-script` id and registers theme cleanup on SPA nav: [lines 40-75](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L40-L75). |
| coding / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR deletes local drawer/CSS/assets and delegates drawer behavior to service `sepo.js`, leaving only config forwarding: [Comments.tsx lines 44-70](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L44-L70). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The inline script shrinks to host normalization, attribute forwarding, script injection, and theme bridging: [comments.inline.ts lines 9-75](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L9-L75). |
| coding / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Scope is limited to comments config/docs plus deletion of now-service-owned drawer assets; PR stat is focused on `README.md`, `quartz.ts`, `Comments.tsx`, `comments.inline.ts`, and removed vendored assets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README now documents service-hosted `sepo.js`, theme env changes, and preview envs: [README lines 140-164](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/README.md#L140-L164). |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The README keeps the user task first: enable comments, required identifiers, then optional behavior and local testing. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | The env table and local Giscus test command remain directly usable: [README lines 142-174](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/README.md#L142-L174). |
| communication / generic | Keep temporary migration notes out of durable docs | pass | 6/6 | The one-time “deploy comment-api#8 first” merge-order note is in the PR body, not added to stable README docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Defaults move to service-hosted `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark`, while theme names remain configurable through `GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME` / `GISCUS_DARK_THEME`: [quartz.ts lines 145-146](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L145-L146). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | New public config is capability-oriented (`GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME`, `GISCUS_DARK_THEME`, preview PR/branch/domain), and the existing `GISCUS_APP_HOST` continues as the service host knob. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 6/7 | Existing enums/list/numeric inputs are validated in `quartz.ts`; new `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are separate fields but only trimmed and forwarded: [lines 111-119](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L111-L119). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 5/7 | `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR` remains numeric-validated and build output emits preview attrs, but branch/domain preview attrs have no local validation or documented service-side validation boundary. |

## Notes

- Verified locally in a detached PR worktree: `npm ci && npm run check` passed, and a full comments-enabled build emitted `.sepo-embed` with all expected attributes plus `postscript.js` injection for `sepo.js`.
- I could not inspect `self-evolving/comment-api#8` because the repository was not accessible to the available token, so service-side idempotence and `window.sepoComments.setTheme()` are scored from the PR contract plus template-side evidence.
- Smallest useful follow-up: either validate `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` locally or state that `sepo.js` owns validation for those forwarded preview fields.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are new preview-facing inputs that are passed through without local validation. This is not blocking for the template handoff, but it leaves the preview contract less explicit than `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #28 is stacked on #27 and moves the comments drawer experience out of the template into service-hosted `sepo.js`. The template now emits a hidden `.sepo-embed` config element and forwards that config onto a script tag loaded from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` on SPA navigation. It also switches default widget themes to `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark` and adds preview branch/domain metadata for the service-owned preview pill. The PR body notes that `self-evolving/comment-api#8` should deploy before this merges; that service repo was not accessible to the available token, so service-side idempotence is evaluated from the stated PR contract.

## Review
The architectural direction is sound and the diff removes a large amount of vendored drawer code in favor of a smaller template integration. There are no BLOCKING findings, but two template-side issues should be fixed or clarified before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Theme changes can be lost during sepo.js load | WARNING | The `themechange` handler only calls `window.sepoComments?.setTheme(...)`, so toggles before `sepo.js` initializes can leave the pending script with stale `data-theme`. |
| Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary | WARNING | `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are trimmed and forwarded without validation or clear service-owned validation docs, unlike `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR`. |
| Duplicated PR-number attributes need confirmation | INFO | `Comments.tsx` emits both `data-pr-number` and `data-preview-pr` for the same value; this may be intentional compatibility, but the consumer boundary is not explained. |

## Progress
- Current PR conversation and review artifacts were inspected, including PR #27 context.
- `check` and Vercel are passing on the current PR head.
- No existing inline comments or review threads were present before synthesis.
- Posted inline comments for `quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts:72` and `quartz.ts:119`.
- Did not resolve or minimize any existing threads or comments.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Theme changes can be lost during sepo.js load</summary>

**Cause:** [`comments.inline.ts:72`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L72) no-ops until `window.sepoComments` exists, while `mountSepo()` already snapshotted `data-theme` before appending the async script.

**Candidate solutions:** Update the pending `#sepo-embed-script` `dataset.theme` inside `onThemeChange` before calling `setTheme`, or re-apply the latest theme from a script load/init path.

**Comments:** One reviewer rated this INFO, but the current code supports the race and the fix is small.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary</summary>

**Cause:** [`quartz.ts:116-119`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz.ts#L116-L119) accepts free-form preview branch/domain values and forwards them into rendered pages.

**Candidate solutions:** Validate the local contract, add a build-time warning for local-only preview-domain usage, or document that `sepo.js` owns validation and clarify in the README that `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN=localhost` is for local testing.

**Comments:** Claude and rubrics review both called out this preview-contract gap.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Duplicated PR-number attributes need confirmation</summary>

**Cause:** [`Comments.tsx:66-67`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/a7c170204c2ed048bb1330ee9dcf0196bd3c079c/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L66-L67) emits both legacy-looking `data-pr-number` and new `data-preview-pr`.

**Candidate solutions:** Keep both with a short compatibility comment naming the consumers, or drop the unused one after confirming the `sepo.js` contract.

**Comments:** This needs service-consumer context, so it is not included as an automated action item.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved WARNING findings are concrete branch changes that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Preserve the latest theme on the pending `sepo.js` script when theme changes happen before `window.sepoComments` initializes.
- [ ] Add validation or explicit documentation for the `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` contract, including local-only `localhost` usage.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 98 | PASS | 12 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make user-facing automation idempotent | pass | 7/7 | [`comments.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L43-L60) reuses the stable `sepo-embed-script` id, removes the prior script before SPA reinjection, and updates pending theme state before `sepo.js` initializes. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | The PR deletes vendored drawer UI/CSS/assets and leaves the template with config emission plus script forwarding: [`Comments.tsx`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L44-L78), [`comments.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L18-L60). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The client script is now only config lookup, host normalization, attribute forwarding, script injection, and theme bridging. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to comments config/docs and removal of now-service-owned drawer assets in the PR file list. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | README documents service-hosted `sepo.js`, new theme variables, tabs, and preview variables: [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/README.md#L140-L164). |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 7/7 | The comments docs lead with enabling comments and required identifiers before explaining optional service behavior. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 7/7 | The env table and local test command remain directly usable: [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/README.md#L142-L174). |
| communication / generic | Keep temporary migration notes out of durable docs | pass | 6/6 | The one-time “deploy comment-api#8 first” ordering note is kept in the PR body, not durable README content. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 8/8 | Defaults move to `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark`, while theme names and service host remain configurable: [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz.ts#L149-L165). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 6/6 | Public knobs describe their capability: `GISCUS_LIGHT_THEME`, `GISCUS_DARK_THEME`, tab fields, content repo, and preview identity fields. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | partial | 6/7 | Enum/list/default-tab and positive PR validation are explicit in [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz.ts#L97-L140); `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are separate fields but remain free-form pass-throughs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate preview contracts | partial | 6/7 | `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR` now rejects non-positive values and warns when `pulls` is disabled, but preview branch/domain validation is still delegated implicitly: [`quartz.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz.ts#L111-L127). |

## Notes

- Current PR head `23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c` addresses the earlier #27/#28 review gaps for positive PR validation, default-tab cross-field validation, duplicate PR attribute explanation, and the pending theme race.
- GitHub checks for PR #28 are passing, including `check`.
- I could not inspect `self-evolving/comment-api#8`; the available token cannot access `self-evolving/comment-api`, so service-side `sepo.js` behavior is evaluated only through the template-side contract.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are forwarded as free-form preview metadata without local validation or an explicit service-owned validation boundary. This is a small remaining gap against the explicit-input and preview-contract rubrics, but not blocking given the template now validates the higher-risk PR number and default-tab combinations.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #28 is stacked on #27 and moves the comments drawer runtime out of the template into service-hosted `sepo.js`.
- The template now emits a hidden `.sepo-embed` config element and forwards that config onto a script loaded from `GISCUS_APP_HOST` on each SPA navigation.
- It also switches default widget themes to `sepo_light` / `sepo_dark`, forwards preview identity metadata, and deletes the vendored drawer UI, styles, themes, and mascot assets.
- The PR pairs with `self-evolving/comment-api#8`; that service repo was not accessible to the available token, so service behavior is evaluated through the stated PR contract and template-side code.

## Review
The direction is sound: the template is much smaller and delegates drawer behavior to the service where it belongs. There are no BLOCKING findings, but two current WARNINGs remain grounded in the live PR head and current review artifacts.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Sepo runtime is not torn down on pages without comments | WARNING | [`comments.inline.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L63-L66) returns when `.sepo-embed` is absent, so SPA navigation from a comments page to a no-comments page can leave the previous service runtime active. |
| Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary | WARNING | [`SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz.ts#L124-L127) are trimmed and forwarded without local validation or README prose that explicitly assigns validation to `sepo.js`. |
| Stale `/static/giscus` headers remain after theme deletion | INFO | The PR deletes `quartz/static/giscus/{light,dark}.css`, but Vercel and local dev still special-case `/static/giscus` CORS headers. This is inert cleanup, not required branch work. |

## Progress
- Current head `23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c` addresses earlier findings for the pending theme race, positive PR validation, default-tab validation, and duplicate PR attribute explanation.
- Posted a new inline comment for the no-config teardown issue at `quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts:65`.
- Existing inline feedback at `quartz.ts:127` already covers the preview branch/domain contract gap, so no duplicate comment was posted.
- Marked the older same-agent theme-race inline comment as outdated; no threads were resolved because the viewer cannot resolve them.
- Review artifacts report `npm run check` and a comments-enabled build passed.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Sepo runtime is not torn down on pages without comments</summary>

**Cause:** The `nav` handler returns immediately when `getConfig()` finds no `.sepo-embed`, while the PR’s cleanup contract says the previous embed is torn down when `sepo.js` is re-injected. On no-comments pages, no re-injection happens.

**Candidate solutions:** Add an explicit no-config teardown path before returning, such as calling a service-owned destroy/cleanup API if available, removing the prior `#sepo-embed-script`, and clearing any service-owned shell/listeners through the supported runtime contract.

**Comments:** Codex raised this as a current-head issue, and an inline comment was posted at https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/28#discussion_r3392365711.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Preview branch/domain inputs need a contract boundary</summary>

**Cause:** `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` and `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` are free-form env values forwarded into the rendered page, while `SEPO_PREVIEW_PR` has local validation and the README only gives a short env-table description.

**Candidate solutions:** Either validate branch/domain shape locally, or add README prose stating that `sepo.js` owns validation for these forwarded preview fields and that `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN=localhost` is intended only for local pill testing.

**Comments:** Claude and rubrics review both flagged this. Existing inline feedback already covers the issue: https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/28#discussion_r3391157161.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Stale `/static/giscus` headers remain after theme deletion</summary>

**Cause:** The deleted local Giscus theme CSS no longer needs the special CORS handling still present in [`vercel.json`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/vercel.json#L7-L16) and [`quartz/cli/handlers.js`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/23b47074595a43521a47ca7cdfbf7cf57e581a5c/quartz/cli/handlers.js#L463-L470).

**Candidate solutions:** Remove the stale header rules in a cleanup pass, or leave them intentionally if future local theme hosting is still expected.

**Comments:** This is an INFO-level leftover from the asset deletion and is not included as required automation work.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The unresolved WARNING findings are localized code/docs changes that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add an explicit teardown path when `.sepo-embed` is absent on SPA navigation so the previous Sepo runtime is cleaned up.
- [ ] Validate or document the `SEPO_PREVIEW_BRANCH` / `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN` contract, including local-only `SEPO_PREVIEW_DOMAIN=localhost` usage.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Load the drawer from the Sepo comments service (sepo.js)
